body.title: One-time Murderbot security review of all platform security decisions
body.result: reviewed all 7 tickets; filed 3 HIGH + 1 MEDIUM gap tickets; wrote docs/security_known_limitations.md with 10 known limitations
body.description:
A comprehensive adversarial security review of all security-related design decisions from the 2026-05-30 platform design session. Run as a CC session prompted to think like Murderbot — a security construct whose job is to find what could go wrong, not confirm what looks right.

Scope — review these tickets for threat vectors, gaps, and bypass scenarios:
- T-provenance-identity (rack-issued tokens — can they be forged, replayed, or stolen?)
- T-provenance-write-attribution (memory attribution — can a bad actor spoof source_token?)
- T-provenance-trust-scoring (trust tiers — can an agent game its tier upward?)
- T-shim-policy-gate (pre-execution gate — what bypasses it? race conditions? cold-start window?)
- T-shim-output-validation (output validation — what evasion is possible?)
- T-agent-kill-switch (kill switch — can a halted agent unhalted itself?)
- T-container-shim (container isolation — what escape vectors exist? loopback-only — is that enough?)

Output: a prioritized list of gaps and weaknesses. Each gap that warrants a fix becomes a ticket. Gaps that are acceptable risks get documented as known limitations.

Reviewer posture: assume the agents are not malicious but can be manipulated via prompt injection. Assume the rack operators are trusted. Assume external agents (ContainerShim tier) are untrusted.

**Affected files:** none — review only; output is tickets + documented known limitations
**Design rules:** none apply to the review itself
**Scope boundary:** IN — review the 7 tickets listed; identify gaps; file fix tickets; document acceptable risks; OUT — fixing the gaps (those get their own tickets)
**Completion criteria:** each of the 7 tickets has been reviewed; any HIGH severity gaps have filed tickets; a short 'known limitations' note is written to docs/security_known_limitations.md
